description: Unlinked public review build of the Audio Jacobian Lens, with full cached explorers, an optional fitted phone-signature view for ASR encoder states, and curated findings pages.
interaction_boundary: The primary pages provide static sample selection, cleared-input playback, synchronized position/layer selection, cell-local hover and keyboard detail, and click-pinned candidate inspection. Every family uses contained horizontal position and layer scrollers whose selected timestep auto-reveals without moving the page. ASR uses exact 100 ms encoder windows with 20 ms overlap and an 80 ms hop, adds a toggleable fitted phone-signature view, and synchronizes its waveform, encoder window, decoder, and HEAD through approximate DTW timing. Speech-to-speech keeps its complete generated-text sequence in one continuous language-layer/HEAD matrix but does not claim alignment to the input waveform. TTS synchronizes its acoustic-code timeline with all fitted T3 layers, the speech HEAD, candidate detail, and selected-position text trace. Phone signatures, TTS diagnostics, and recorded intervention inspection remain cached interactions. No model inference, upload, recording, generation, or intervention endpoint is present.
media_policy: Only the ten CC BY 4.0 LibriSpeech input FLACs are included. Generated LFM and Chatterbox audio remains excluded pending derived-output review.
payload_policy: Each detailed family manifest pins model/lens provenance and hashes ten reduced reports. ASR uses a speaker-held-out A2 encoder lens plus frozen train-only phone prototypes; all encoder reports were recomputed at five-state 100 ms pooling with one-state overlap and a 100-bin safety limit. Reports publish only five bounded phone candidates per encoder cell and exclude prototype tensors, occurrence-level rows, private paths, ephemeral server IDs, and embedded audio. Legacy character-length sidecars remain hash-pinned for reproducibility but are no longer exposed or loaded by the Explorer.
